Article.ArticleTitle: Valorization of waste R227ea refrigerant into fuels and chemicals via supercritical water gasification: A ReaxFF-MD and DFT study.

Abstract.AbstractText: Escalating ozone depletion and global warming concerns have positioned the refrigeration and air conditioning sector as a critical arena for climate-change mitigation. High-market-share refrigerants, particularly hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) recognized as greenhouse gases, are being progressively regulated due to their high global warming potentials, rendering the safe treatment and valorization of end-of-life HFCs an urgent challenge. Supercritical water gasification (SCWG) is capable of efficiently converting organic wastes into value-added products and therefore represents a promising route for HFC abatement. Herein, R227ea (1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane) was selected as a representative HFC to elucidate its degradation mechanism under SCWG conditions. A combined ReaxFF reactive molecular dynamics and density functional theory framework was employed to resolve elementary pathways and assess the effects of temperature, pressure, and reactant concentration on product formation. The results show that HF, CO, and H2 are the major products under the investigated conditions, with more than 95% of fluorine atoms converted into HF. Elevated temperature markedly accelerates R227ea decomposition and, within an appropriate range, promotes the formation of HF, CO, and H2. Increasing the H2O concentration enhances H2 and CO2 production while suppressing CO formation. Notably, HF formation constitutes the kinetically favored pathway for the R227ea-H2O reaction network. Overall, this study advances mechanistic understanding of HFC destruction in supercritical water and supports SCWG as a potential strategy for converting waste HFCs into value-added products.
